slides.title: Every script you automate is a *liability* you now own.
slides.body: We only ever count the asset.

slides.title: Automating feels like pure win
slides.body: Do it once, it runs itself. Close the ticket. Move on.

slides.title: What nobody logs
slides.body: Software you now maintain and trust for life — or it becomes a *hazard*.

slides.title: The interest it accrues
steps: Breaks on every vendor API change

slides.title: Manual work degrades gracefully
slides.body: A human notices when something's off.

slides.title: Unmaintained automation has a *trapdoor*
slides.body: It fails silently, at scale, on stale assumptions.

slides.title: The uncomfortable math
slides.body:
Automation you don't maintain isn't saving time.
It's *deferring a bill*.

slides.title: Automate less. Own what you automate.
slides.body:
A little well-maintained beats a graveyard of scripts.

Follow *@mzsnetworks*
